import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  mut have = false
  mut neg = false
  mut cur = 0
  while i < n
    let b = match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(v)
      v
    when None
      0
    end
    if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 13 or b == 10
      if have
        let val = if neg
          -cur
        else
          cur
        end
        result = list.push(result, val)
        have = false
        neg = false
        cur = 0
      end
    else if b == 45
      neg = true
      have = true
    else
      cur = cur * 10 + (b - 48)
      have = true
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if have
    let val = if neg
      -cur
    else
      cur
    end
    result = list.push(result, val)
  end
  result
end

fn bsearch(_ xs: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = list.len(xs)
  while lo < hi
    let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
    let v = match list.get(xs, mid)
    when Some(x)
      x
    when None
      0
    end
    if v == t
      return Some(mid)
    else if v < t
      lo = mid + 1
    else
      hi = mid
    end
  end
  None
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let line1 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let line2 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let xs = parse_ints(line1)
  let target = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
  when Some(n)
    n
  when None
    0
  end
  match bsearch(xs, target)
  when Some(i)
    println("index=#{i}")
  when None
    println("not_found")
  end
end
